Class LegoSetManagerTest

java.lang.Object
LegoSetManagerTest
class LegoSetManagerTest extends Object
LegoSetManagerTest class contains unit tests for the LegoSetManager. Verifies correct functionality for adding, deleting, listing, updating, and loading data from files.

  • Field Summary

    Description
    (package private) LegoSet

  • Method Details

    • setUp

      @BeforeEach void setUp()

    • addSet

      @Test @DisplayName("Add lego set to database") void addSet()
      Tests that a Lego set can be successfully added to the manager.

    • addDuplicateSet

      @Test @DisplayName("Verify duplicate ID can not be saved to the database") void addDuplicateSet()
      Tests that duplicate set IDs are not allowed.

    • deleteSet

      @Test @DisplayName("Remove set from database using setID") void deleteSet()
      Tests that a Lego set can be removed using its ID

    • deleteSetNoSetID

      @Test @DisplayName("Delete set - reject non-existent setID") void deleteSetNoSetID()
      Tests deletion with a non-existent set ID

    • listSetsEmpty

      @Test @DisplayName("List set - before any data is loaded") void listSetsEmpty()
      Tests listing sets when no data has been added.

    • listSets

      @Test @DisplayName("List Sets - Verify data is saved and displayed") void listSets()
      Tests that stored Lego Sets are returned correctly.

    • findSet

      @Test @DisplayName("Find Set - Set found using setID") void findSet()
      Tests that a Lego Set can be found using its ID.

    • findSetNoSetID

      @Test @DisplayName("Set can not be found because setID does not exist") void findSetNoSetID()
      Tests searching for a non-existing Lego set ID.

    • updateAttribute

      @Test @DisplayName("Update pieceCount with valid value") void updateAttribute()
      Test updating a Lego set with a valid piece count.

    • updateAttributedatavalidation

      @Test @DisplayName("Reject invalid pieceCount input ( non-numeric)") void updateAttributedatavalidation()
      Tests updating a lego set with an invalid piece count input.

    • countTotalPieces

      @Test @DisplayName("Count total pieces - expect piece count to match 9023") void countTotalPieces()
      Tests the total piece count calculation.

    • countTotalPiecesNoSets

      @Test @DisplayName("Expect pieces count to be zero per no sets have been added") void countTotalPiecesNoSets()
      Tests total piece count with no data added.

    • loadSetsFromFile

      @Test void loadSetsFromFile()
      Tests loading Lego sets from a valid file.

    • loadSetsFromFileIncorrectPath

      @Test void loadSetsFromFileIncorrectPath()
      Tests loading Lego sets from an invalid file path.
